2026-04-20T07:26:31.1671597Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T07:26:31.1672144Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T07:26:31.1672563Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T07:26:31.1672924Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T07:26:31.1673227Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T07:26:31.1673463Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T07:26:31.1673625Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-20T07:26:31.1674668Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-20T07:26:31.1675461Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). 2026-04-20T07:30:07.2787935Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T07:27:36.704170061Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58364,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: document eager previous_response_id validation and ResourceNotFoundException.Code/Param\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58347 by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nBeta.3 introduced two significant behavioral changes:\\n\\n1. **Eager validation of `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id`** — the library now resolves these references against the storage provider *before* invoking the handler. Invalid `previous_response_id` returns HTTP 404 immediately; invalid `conversation.id` returns HTTP 400. Previously, these errors were silently ignored.\\n\\n2. **Richer storage error propagation** — `ResourceNotFoundException` gained two new public properties, `Code` and `Param`, mirroring `BadRequestException`. `StorageErrorMapper` now extracts the full Foundry error body (`code`, `message`, `param`, `type`) and propagates it through the exception hierarchy. Non-400/404/409 upstream errors are normalized to HTTP 500 instead of being proxied as-is.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Troubleshooting** section documents the 404 case only as \\u0026#34;The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL.\\u0026#34; It must also document the new 404 scenario: invalid `previous_response_id` detected during eager validation, and the matching 400 for an invalid `conversation.id`.\\n- The **`ResponseContext`** description for `GetHistoryAsync()` does not mention that history/conversation ID resolution now happens eagerly before the handler is invoked, so handlers will never see a lazy 404 mid-stream from those calls. This is important for handler authors to understand.\\n- There is no documentation (in the README or in `docs/handler-implementation-guide.md`) explaining the new `Code` and `Param` properties on `ResourceNotFoundException`, nor how provider implementers should populate them when throwing this exception.\\n- The README has no mention of the new guarantee that non-4xx upstream storage errors are normalized to HTTP 500, which affects how operators reason about error codes surfaced to clients.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Edit\\n\\n**A. Update the `ResponseContext` bullet for `GetHistoryAsync`**\\n\\nLocate the bullet:\\n```\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100).\\n```\\n\\nReplace with:\\n```\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100). The library validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` against the provider *before* invoking the handler — invalid references return HTTP 404 or HTTP 400 immediately, and the resolved result is cached so handlers calling `GetHistoryAsync()` reuse it without a second storage lookup.\\n```\\n\\n**B. Update the Troubleshooting section**\\n\\nLocate the `### Common errors` section and add two new bullets after the existing 404 entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **404 Not Found** (create): The `previous_response_id` field in the request does not match any existing response. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (create): The `conversation.id` field in the request is invalid or references an unknown conversation. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked.\\n```\\n\\nAlso update the existing entry:\\n```\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL.\\n```\\nto:\\n```\\n- **404 Not Found** (get/cancel/delete): The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL.\\n```\\n\\n**C. Add a note about upstream error normalization in Troubleshooting**\\n\\nAfter the existing common errors list, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Storage error propagation\\n\\nWhen the library calls Foundry storage and receives a structured error response, it forwards the full error body — `code`, `message`, `param`, and `type` — to the client for 400, 404, and 409 responses. All other upstream error status codes are normalized to HTTP 500 to prevent leaking infrastructure details.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Edit\\n\\nFind the section that describes exception types thrown from provider implementations (if present). Add documentation for the updated `ResourceNotFoundException`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n#### ResourceNotFoundException\\n\\nThrow `ResourceNotFoundException` from a provider method when a requested resource (response, input item, etc.) does not exist. The library maps this to HTTP 404.\\n\\n```\\nthrow new ResourceNotFoundException(\\n    message: \\u0026#34;Response not found.\\u0026#34;,\\n    code: \\u0026#34;not_found\\u0026#34;,        // Optional: structured error code forwarded to the client\\n    param: \\u0026#34;response_id\\u0026#34;);    // Optional: the field name that caused the error\\n```\\n\\nThe `Code` and `Param` properties (added in 1.0.0-beta.3) are forwarded verbatim in the HTTP error response body, mirroring the `BadRequestException` API.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for these changes — the Troubleshooting and provider exception guidance does not need live code samples. Existing snippets remain valid.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections in order:\\n1. Getting started ✅ (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. Key concepts ✅\\n3. Examples ✅\\n4. Troubleshooting ✅ — needs the additions described in Step 1\\n5. Next steps ✅\\n6. Contributing ✅\\n\\nNo structural changes are needed; only the content additions described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no README changes (no new snippet regions were added)\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes are expected for these doc edits\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; Invalid `previous_response_id` returns HTTP 404 immediately; invalid `conversation.id` returns HTTP 400. Previously, these errors were silently ignored.\\n\\n2. **Richer storage error propagation** — `ResourceNotFoundException` gained two new public properties, `Code` and `Param`, mirroring `BadRequestException`. `StorageErrorMapper` now extracts the full Foundry error body (`code`, `message`, `param`, `type`) and propagates it through the exception hierarchy. Non-400/404/409 upstream errors are normalized to HTTP 500 instead of being proxied as-is.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Troubleshooting** section documents the 404 case only as \\u0026#34;The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL.\\u0026#34; It must also document the new 404 scenario: invalid `previous_response_id` detected during eager validation, and the matching 400 for an invalid `conversation.id`.\\n- The **`ResponseContext`** description for `GetHistoryAsync()` does not mention that history/conversation ID resolution now happens eagerly before the handler is invoked, so handlers will never see a lazy 404 mid-stream from those calls. This is important for handler authors to understand.\\n- There is no documentation (in the README or in `docs/handler-implementation-guide.md`) explaining the new `Code` and `Param` properties on `ResourceNotFoundException`, nor how provider implementers should populate them when throwing this exception.\\n- The README has no mention of the new guarantee that non-4xx upstream storage errors are normalized to HTTP 500, which affects how operators reason about error codes surfaced to clients.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Edit\\n\\n**A. Update the `ResponseContext` bullet for `GetHistoryAsync`**\\n\\nLocate the bullet:\\n```\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100).\\n```\\n\\nReplace with:\\n```\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100). The library validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` against the provider *before* invoking the handler — invalid references return HTTP 404 or HTTP 400 immediately, and the resolved result is cached so handlers calling `GetHistoryAsync()` reuse it without a second storage lookup.\\n```\\n\\n**B. Update the Troubleshooting section**\\n\\nLocate the `### Common errors` section and add two new bullets after the existing 404 entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **404 Not Found** (create): The `previous_response_id` field in the request does not match any existing response. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (create): The `conversation.id` field in the request is invalid or references an unknown conversation. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked.\\n```\\n\\nAlso update the existing entry:\\n```\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL.\\n```\\nto:\\n```\\n- **404 Not Found** (get/cancel/delete): The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL.\\n```\\n\\n**C. Add a note about upstream error normalization in Troubleshooting**\\n\\nAfter the existing common errors list, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Storage error propagation\\n\\nWhen the library calls Foundry storage and receives a structured error response, it forwards the full error body — `code`, `message`, `param`, and `type` — to the client for 400, 404, and 409 responses. All other upstream error status codes are normalized to HTTP 500 to prevent leaking infrastructure details.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Edit\\n\\nFind the section that describes exception types thrown from provider implementations (if present). Add documentation for the updated `ResourceNotFoundException`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n#### ResourceNotFoundException\\n\\nThrow `ResourceNotFoundException` from a provider method when a requested resource (response, input item, etc.) does not exist. The library maps this to HTTP 404.\\n\\n```\\nthrow new ResourceNotFoundException(\\n    message: \\u0026#34;Response not found.\\u0026#34;,\\n    code: \\u0026#34;not_found\\u0026#34;,        // Optional: structured error code forwarded to the client\\n    param: \\u0026#34;response_id\\u0026#34;);    // Optional: the field name that caused the error\\n```\\n\\nThe `Code` and `Param` properties (added in 1.0.0-beta.3) are forwarded verbatim in the HTTP error response body, mirroring the `BadRequestException` API.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for these changes — the Troubleshooting and provider exception guidance does not need live code samples. Existing snippets remain valid.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections in order:\\n1. Getting started ✅ (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. Key concepts ✅\\n3. Examples ✅\\n4. Troubleshooting ✅ — needs the additions described in Step 1\\n5. Next steps ✅\\n6. Contributing ✅\\n\\nNo structural changes are needed; only the content additions described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no README changes (no new snippet regions were added)\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes are expected for these doc edits\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-20T07:30:07.2939174Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T07:27:54.999437099Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58364,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: document eager previous_response_id validation and ResourceNotFoundException.Code/Param\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58347 by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nBeta.3 introduced two significant behavioral changes:\\n\\n1. **Eager validation of `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id`** — the library now resolves these references against the storage provider *before* invoking the handler. Invalid `previous_response_id` returns HTTP 404 immediately; invalid `conversation.id` returns HTTP 400. Previously, these errors were silently ignored.\\n\\n2. **Richer storage error propagation** — `ResourceNotFoundException` gained two new public properties, `Code` and `Param`, mirroring `BadRequestException`. `StorageErrorMapper` now extracts the full Foundry error body (`code`, `message`, `param`, `type`) and propagates it through the exception hierarchy. Non-400/404/409 upstream errors are normalized to HTTP 500 instead of being proxied as-is.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Troubleshooting** section documents the 404 case only as \\u0026#34;The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL.\\u0026#34; It must also document the new 404 scenario: invalid `previous_response_id` detected during eager validation, and the matching 400 for an invalid `conversation.id`.\\n- The **`ResponseContext`** description for `GetHistoryAsync()` does not mention that history/conversation ID resolution now happens eagerly before the handler is invoked, so handlers will never see a lazy 404 mid-stream from those calls. This is important for handler authors to understand.\\n- There is no documentation (in the README or in `docs/handler-implementation-guide.md`) explaining the new `Code` and `Param` properties on `ResourceNotFoundException`, nor how provider implementers should populate them when throwing this exception.\\n- The README has no mention of the new guarantee that non-4xx upstream storage errors are normalized to HTTP 500, which affects how operators reason about error codes surfaced to clients.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Edit\\n\\n**A. Update the `ResponseContext` bullet for `GetHistoryAsync`**\\n\\nLocate the bullet:\\n```\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100).\\n```\\n\\nReplace with:\\n```\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100). The library validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` against the provider *before* invoking the handler — invalid references return HTTP 404 or HTTP 400 immediately, and the resolved result is cached so handlers calling `GetHistoryAsync()` reuse it without a second storage lookup.\\n```\\n\\n**B. Update the Troubleshooting section**\\n\\nLocate the `### Common errors` section and add two new bullets after the existing 404 entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **404 Not Found** (create): The `previous_response_id` field in the request does not match any existing response. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (create): The `conversation.id` field in the request is invalid or references an unknown conversation. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked.\\n```\\n\\nAlso update the existing entry:\\n```\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL.\\n```\\nto:\\n```\\n- **404 Not Found** (get/cancel/delete): The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL.\\n```\\n\\n**C. Add a note about upstream error normalization in Troubleshooting**\\n\\nAfter the existing common errors list, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Storage error propagation\\n\\nWhen the library calls Foundry storage and receives a structured error response, it forwards the full error body — `code`, `message`, `param`, and `type` — to the client for 400, 404, and 409 responses. All other upstream error status codes are normalized to HTTP 500 to prevent leaking infrastructure details.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Edit\\n\\nFind the section that describes exception types thrown from provider implementations (if present). Add documentation for the updated `ResourceNotFoundException`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n#### ResourceNotFoundException\\n\\nThrow `ResourceNotFoundException` from a provider method when a requested resource (response, input item, etc.) does not exist. The library maps this to HTTP 404.\\n\\n```\\nthrow new ResourceNotFoundException(\\n    message: \\u0026#34;Response not found.\\u0026#34;,\\n    code: \\u0026#34;not_found\\u0026#34;,        // Optional: structured error code forwarded to the client\\n    param: \\u0026#34;response_id\\u0026#34;);    // Optional: the field name that caused the error\\n```\\n\\nThe `Code` and `Param` properties (added in 1.0.0-beta.3) are forwarded verbatim in the HTTP error response body, mirroring the `BadRequestException` API.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for these changes — the Troubleshooting and provider exception guidance does not need live code samples.
